items.briefing:
Australia's Signals Directorate (ASD) has issued a warning about a phishing technique called "device code phishing," where criminals trick users into handing over account access by getting them to enter a code on a legitimate-looking login page. Security researchers are also tracking an increase in criminal toolkits that make this type of attack easier to carry out at scale — meaning more Australians could be targeted. Separately, GitHub has confirmed a breach affecting around 4,000 internal repositories, which may have exposed code or credentials relevant to developers and organisations using the platform.

If you receive an unexpected email or message asking you to enter a code on a website to verify your identity or sign in, stop and verify the request through official channels before proceeding.

featured.title: ASD warns of device code phishing as Proofpoint tracks growing criminal toolkits
featured.quote: ASD warning on device code phishing targets millions of Australian Microsoft 365 users directly.
